title: Grants Pass favorite spots
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-August-2020 list of nine Grants Pass hangout spots Yahir was rotating through during senior-year COVID lockdown — outdoor tables, parking-lot edges, park benches.
  • Sites: corner of Sizzler's, table adjacent to Performant, outer tables at Tussing Park, lower table at Riverside Park "Lola Spot", parking lot behind MyBookStore, behind Walmart Auto Center, Westholm Park, the run path to Grange Co-op bench, table at Lawn Ridge League.
  • "Riverside Park Lola Spot" — first "Lola" mention in vault may predate the Lola the car entity; ambiguity worth flagging — this might be a place called Lola Spot, not the car.
  • The list reads as a pandemic era response to closed indoor spaces — Yahir cataloging the outdoor surfaces of Grants Pass he could legally be at during summer-2020 lockdown.
  • Continuously updated through Aug-2021 (last-modified date) — this is a year-long living document, not a one-shot capture; the rotating-spots habit persisted into Yahir's last summer before OSU.

Note

A geography of restless. Yahir at 17 mapping the perimeter of Grants Pass he could think/work/be-alone in during pandemic restrictions — Sizzler's parking corner, the Performant office-park edge, the Riverside bench he kept calling "Lola Spot." This is the GP teen era version of the spatial inventory Yahir would later make leaving Austin — the same instinct (name your places before you leave them) at both endpoints of the same arc. The spots cluster around the river and the commercial spine of GP, sketching the actual physical scope of Yahir's pre-college life.

Connections

  • grants pass or · grants pass teen era
  • covid pandemic era — context for outdoor-table list
  • tussing park · riverside park · westholm park — first vault mentions
  • 2025 08 21 austin goodbye packlist — spiritual companion, opposite endpoint
  • lola — possible Lola-the-car ambiguity
